schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: a$ and $a + 76800 \pmod{153600}$ correspond to diametrically opposite points on the sphere. \subsection{Double Helix Geometry and Differential Properties} The optical propagation medium consists of a rotating double helix structure parametrized by arc length and rotation angle. This geometry combines helical spatial structure with temporal rotation to create a four-dimensional manifold of light paths. \begin{definition}[Rotating Double Helix Parametrization] Let $\Omega$ denote the angular rotation velocity, $R$ the helix radius, and $P$ the helical pitch. The two strands are parametrized as: \begin{align} \mathbf{s}_1(t, \Omega) &= \mathcal{R}(\Omega t) \cdot \begin{pmatrix} R\cos(2\pi t/P) \\ R\sin(2\pi t/P) \\ t \end{pmatrix}\\ \mathbf{s}_2(t, \Omega) &= \mathcal{R}(\Omega t) \cdot \begin{pmatrix} R\cos(2\pi t/P + \pi) \\ R\sin(2\pi t/P + \pi) \\ t \end{pmatrix} \end{align} where $\mathcal{R}(\theta)$ denotes the rotation matrix about the $z$-axis through angle $\theta$. \end{definition} The differential geometric properties of these curves determine the optical characteristics of light propagation. The tangent vectors $\mathbf{T}_i = d\mathbf{s}_i/dt$ define the instantaneous d
theory.technical_interpretation: a a + 76800 {153600} R P {R}( ) z {T}_i = d {s}_i/dt$ define the instantaneous d It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side.
